statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function that finds the first occurrence of a target element in an array. The function should return the index of the target element if it exists, otherwise return -1. The function should not mutate the original array.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes two parameters: an array of integers and a target integer.
statement.outputFormat: The function returns an integer representing the index of the target element if it exists, otherwise returns -1.
statement.notes: The function should not use the built-in indexOf method. Instead, it should implement a manual search algorithm.

examples.explanation: The function should return the index of the target element 3, which is 2.
The function should return -1 since the target element 6 does not exist in the array.

hints.text: Use a manual search algorithm to find the target element.

editorial.title: Editorial: Finding the First Occurrence of an Element
editorial.intuition: The function should iterate through the array and check each element to see if it matches the target element.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to use a manual search algorithm that iterates through the array and checks each element.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a manual search algorithm that iterates through the array and checks each element.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the function returns the index of the target element if it exists, otherwise returns -1.
commonMistakes: Not handling the edge case where the target element does not exist
